what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment that points to an external image asset associated with a design-source channel memory entry.
observations: The file consists solely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an attachment id att-a333786d, channel design-source, and attachment_type images.
The target field uses a URI-like value image://design-source/company-info/p-12.
standouts: The target uses an image:// URI scheme, treating the attachment as a pointer rather than an embedded payload.
The file is a minimal provenance stub with a precise timestamp.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Follows the att- prefixed id convention and channel-memory attachment directory layout.
References an external asset by URI instead of inlining binary content.
concerns: No content hash, size, or MIME type is recorded for the referenced image.
No uploader, author, or source context is provided.
The resolvability and location of image://design-source/company-info/p-12 cannot be verified from this file alone.
notes: This is a lightweight, timestamped provenance stub that links a design-source channel memory to an external image asset via a URI.
questions: Who added this attachment and from what source?
What does the referenced image p-12 depict?
Is the image:// URI scheme resolvable by the consuming system?
Is attachment_type being plural ('images') intentional for a single image reference?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose as an attachment metadata record matches its actual content: it stores a typed, timestamped reference to an external image.
apparent_themes: Separation of metadata from media payload
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a durable, timestamped link between a channel memory and an associated image asset without embedding the image data.
contribution: Provides a minimal, timestamped attachment record that links a design-source channel memory to an external image asset, supporting later retrieval and provenance tracing.
summary_for_embedding: Markdown attachment metadata file att-a333786d in the design-source channel records a reference to image asset image://design-source/company-info/p-12 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persist a dated pointer from a design-source channel memory to an associated image attachment.
